content: A canal supplies water to an area growing wheat over 100 hectares. The duration between the first and last watering is 120 days, and the total depth of water required by the crop is 35 cm. The most intense watering is required over a period of 30 days and requires a total depth of water equal to 12 cm. Assuming precipitation to be negligible and neglecting all losses, the minimum discharge (in m3 /s, rounded off to three decimal places) in the canal to satisfy the crop requirement is __________________.
explanation:
Given data :

Area = 100 hectares

Total depth of water, ∆2 = 35 cm

Total time period, B2 = 120 days

Kor depth, ∆1 = 12 cm

Kor period, B1 = 30 days

(i) Kor duty :

The Kor duty is calculated as follows :

$$\rm \Delta_1 = \frac{8.64 \times B_1}{\Delta_1} = \frac{8.64 \times 30}{0.12} = 2160$$ ha/m3/sec

Corresponding discharge can be calculated by dividing the area by the duty :

$$\rm \frac{Area}{Duty} = \frac{100}{2160} = 0.046$$ m3/sec

(ii) Overall duty :

The overall duty is calculated as follows :

$$\rm \Delta_2 = \frac{8.64 \times B_2}{\Delta_2} = \frac{8.64 \times 120}{0.35} = 2962.28$$ ha/m3/sec

Corresponding discharge can be calculated by dividing the area by the duty :

$$\rm \frac{Area}{Duty} = \frac{100}{2962.28} = 0.034$$ m3/sec

The minimum discharge required will be the maximum of the above two values :

∴ Qreq = 0.046 m3/sec

content: In a certain month, the reference crop evapotranspiration at a location is 6 mm day. If the crop coefficient and soil coefficient are 1.2 and 0.8, respectively, the actual evapotranspiration in mm/day is
explanation:
Given :

Reference crop evapotranspiration, $ E_t0 = 6 $ mm/day

Crop coefficient, $ K_c = 1.2 $

Soil coefficient, $ K_s = 0.8 $

The actual evapotranspiration $ E_t $ can be calculated using the formula :

$ E_t = K_c \times K_s \times E_t0 $

Substituting the given values :

$ E_t = 1.2 \times 0.8 \times 6 $

Performing the multiplication :

$ E_t = 5.76 \text{ mm/day} $

Therefore, the actual evapotranspiration is 5.76 mm/day.

content: During a particular stage of the growth of a crop, the consumptive use of water is 2.8 mm/day. The amount of water available in the soil is 50% of the maximum depth of available water in the root zone. Consider the maximum root zone depth of the crop as 80 mm and the irrigation efficiency as 70%. The interval between irrigation (in days) will be ___________. (round off to the nearest integer)
explanation:
Consumption use, $C_u=2.8 \mathrm{~mm} /$ day

Irrigation efficiency $=70 \%$

Depth of root zone $=80 \mathrm{~mm}$

Depth of Irrigation provide $=50 \%$ of $80 \mathrm{~mm}=$ 40 mm

Frequency period $=\frac{40}{2.8}=14.28$ day $\approx 14$ days

content: If duty (D) is 1428 hectares/cumec and base period (B) is 120 days for an irrigated crop, then delta ($$\Delta$$) in meters is given by

A pump with an efficiency of 80% is used to draw groundwater from a well for irrigating a flat field of area 108 hectares. The base period and delta for paddy crop on this field are 120 days and 144 cm, respectively. Water application efficiency in the field is 80%. The lowest level of water in the well is 10 m below the ground. The minimum required horse power (h.p.) of the pump is _________. (round off to two decimal places)

(Consider 1 h.p. = 746 W; unit weight of water = 9810 N/m3
explanation:
Given :

1 h.p. = 746 W

Unit weight of water = 9810 N/m3

Calculation Steps

Discharge Required by the Crop :

$ \frac{A}{Q} = 8.64 \frac{B}{\Delta} $

Where :

$A = 108 \text{ hectares}$

$\Delta = 1.44 \text{ meters}$

$B = 120 \text{ days}$

Solving for $Q$ :

$ Q = \frac{A \Delta}{8.64 B} = \frac{108 \times 1.44}{8.64 \times 120} = 0.15 \text{ m}^3/\text{s} $

Water Required to be Pumped :

$ Q_{\text{req}} = \frac{Q}{\text{Efficiency in the field}} = \frac{0.15}{0.8} = 0.1875 \text{ m}^3/\text{s} $

Power Required by the Pump :

$ \text{Power} = \frac{\gamma \cdot Q_{\text{req}} \cdot H}{\text{Pump efficiency}} $

Where:

$\gamma = 9810 \text{ N/m}^3$ (Specific weight of water)

$Q_{\text{req}} = 0.1875 \text{ m}^3/\text{s}$

$H = 10 \text{ meters}$

Pump efficiency = 0.8

Substituting the values :

$ \text{Power} = \frac{9810 \times 0.1875 \times 10}{0.8 \times 746} = 30.82 \text{ h.p.} $

Thus, the minimum required horsepower (h.p.) of the pump is 30.82 h.p.
